title: Mastering the Low Block
summary: How to frustrate high-possession teams with a compact defensive structure.
fullText: The low block is a defensive strategy where a team defends deep in their own half, leaving very little space behind the defense. To master this, your players must maintain horizontal compactness, shifting as a unit to the side where the ball is. It requires extreme discipline and concentration, as one lapse can allow a through ball. The goal is to force the opponent to play wide and deliver crosses, which are easier to defend with physically dominant center-backs.

title: Exploiting the Half-Spaces
summary: Using the 'corridors' between the wing and the center to create chaos.
fullText: Half-spaces are the vertical strips of the pitch located between the wings and the center. Defenders often struggle to decide who should mark a player in this zone. By positioning creative midfielders or 'inverted' wingers in these pockets, you can pull center-backs out of position or force fullbacks to tuck in, leaving space wide. This is a key component of modern positional play.

title: Gegenpressing Fundamentals
summary: The art of 'counter-pressing' immediately after losing possession.
fullText: Gegenpressing (Counter-pressing) is the tactic of pressing the opponent immediately after losing the ball. The logic is that the opponent is most vulnerable right when they win the ball, as they are transitioning into an attacking shape and haven't yet secured the ball. By swarming the ball carrier instantly, you can win it back high up the pitch and catch them out of position.

title: The Overlapping Fullback
summary: Creating numerical advantages in wide areas with marauding defenders.
fullText: When a winger cuts inside, they drag the opposing fullback with them. This creates a vacuum of space on the flank. An overlapping run from your fullback into this space creates a 2v1 situation. Accurate crossing from these advanced positions is vital for creating high-quality scoring chances.

title: Vertical Tiki-Taka
summary: A faster, more direct version of traditional possession football.
fullText: Unlike the slow, horizontal possession of traditional Tiki-Taka, the vertical version focuses on quick, forward-thinking passes. The aim is to move the ball through the lines as quickly as possible. It requires players with exceptional vision and first-touch ability. The 'one-two' pass is the most effective weapon in this strategy.

title: Defending against the Counter
summary: Preventing 'cheap' goals when your team is attacking high up.
fullText: When you attack with many players, you are vulnerable to long balls. A 'rest defense' is essential. This means your defensive midfielders and remaining defenders must stay balanced even while you're attacking. They must be ready to intercept clearances or commit 'tactical fouls' (within reason) to stop a fast break before it starts.
